name: Credit Helpers
description_short: Credit Helpers is a credit repair service offering bureau disputes, personalized coaching, and credit monitoring to help clients remove inaccurate negative items and improve their scores.
description_long:
Credit Helpers is a credit repair company that assists consumers in identifying and disputing inaccurate, incomplete, or unverifiable information on their credit reports. Founded with a focus on personalized client service, the company works with each client to analyze their credit reports from all three major bureaus — Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion — and develop a targeted dispute strategy based on their individual credit profile. Their team of credit specialists handles communication with bureaus and creditors on the client's behalf, reducing the burden on consumers who may be unfamiliar with the formal dispute process.

Credit Helpers operates on a monthly service model that begins with a free initial consultation and credit report review. Once enrolled, clients receive ongoing bureau disputes, cease and desist letters to stop harassing collector contact, and debt validation letters sent to creditors challenging the accuracy of reported debts. Clients track their case progress through an online portal and have access to a personal credit advisor who provides guidance and answers questions throughout the engagement.

The company distinguishes itself through an emphasis on credit education alongside active dispute work. Credit Helpers provides resources to help clients understand how credit scoring works, which factors most influence their scores, and how to maintain healthy credit habits after the repair process concludes. This educational component is particularly valuable for consumers who have encountered recurring credit difficulties and want to build sustainable financial health rather than a one-time score boost. The Premium tier extends this further with goodwill letter campaigns targeting late payments from otherwise positive account histories.

As with any credit repair service, results depend heavily on the nature of negative items on a client's report. Credit Helpers is best suited for consumers with documented inaccuracies or legally challengeable entries — those with exclusively accurate recent negative information may find limited benefit. The company does not offer a money-back guarantee, which adds some financial risk compared to competitors that do. Budget-conscious consumers should weigh the setup fee and monthly cost against lower-cost alternatives or DIY dispute options before committing.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. Credit Helpers does not guarantee specific score improvements or the removal of any particular item from your credit reports.

tiers.name: Basic
features: Credit report analysis and error identification

tiers.name: Standard
features: All three bureau disputes per cycle
Cease and desist letters to collectors

pros: Free initial consultation with no obligation to enroll
Works with all three major credit bureaus simultaneously on Standard and Premium plans
Goodwill letter campaigns included in Premium tier for late payment removal
Online portal allows real-time dispute progress tracking
Cease and desist and debt validation letters included in Standard plan
Credit education resources help clients build lasting financial habits
cons: No money-back guarantee increases financial risk for skeptical consumers
Not BBB accredited, which may concern some consumers doing due diligence
Setup fee of $99 adds to the upfront cost of getting started
No mobile app — account management is web portal only
Identity theft protection not available on any plan
best_for: Consumers with documented inaccuracies or unverifiable items on their credit reports
People who want hands-on bureau dispute management without doing it themselves
Clients who value credit education alongside active repair services
Those seeking goodwill letter support for past late payments on otherwise positive accounts
diagnosis: Best for consumers with verifiable credit report errors who want full-service bureau dispute management. Not ideal for those requiring a money-back guarantee or identity theft protection coverage.
services: Credit report analysis and negative item identification
Dispute submissions to all three credit bureaus
Cease and desist letters to debt collectors
Goodwill letter campaigns for late payment removal
Credit monitoring with score change alerts
Online portal with real-time case tracking
Credit education and financial wellness resources
typical_results_timeline: Initial dispute responses typically arrive within 30-45 days of submission. Meaningful score improvements generally occur within 3-6 months, with complex cases involving multiple negative items potentially requiring 6-12 months of active service.
